concerns.text: Task T012a mandates fetching CDC FluView data from a GitHub NAB repository URL ('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/numenta/NAB/master/...'). This violates FR-001 and Constitution Principle VI (Surveillance Data Provenance), which require downloading from the 'canonical CDC source'. Using a third-party mirror weakens the data provenance constraint and risks reproducibility if the mirror changes or disappears.

Task T012b allows a fallback to a 'local data/raw/ground_truth_events.csv provided by the user'. This violates FR-006 (ground truth must be sourced from independent CDC Virological/Hospitalization data) and Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth). Allowing user-provided local files introduces the risk of hand-typed or biased ground truth, breaking the auditability and independence constraints.

Task T019 instructs recalculating the Bonferroni threshold as 'min(0.01/N, 1/permutations)' when reducing permutations due to time limits. This is a semantic weakening of FR-004, which mandates a strict Bonferroni correction of 'p < 0.01 / N'. The permutation count affects the *accuracy* of the p-value estimation, not the *threshold* itself. Changing the threshold logic violates the statistical constraint.

Task T008 states the synthetic data generator 'must not be used for final report; reference E-NO-DATA fallback'. While it mentions the fallback, it does not explicitly enforce the 'halt' behavior required by the plan and Constitution Principle VI if real data is missing. The task description is ambiguous about whether the pipeline *must* halt or just *can* use synthetic data for testing, risking a silent relaxation of the 'real data required' constraint.
